visa_types.overview:
Overview
In order to protect Thai nationals who wish to work overseas and prevent the situation where Thai nationals are brought to work overseas without payment, according to the Thai Labour Act B.E. 2528 article 48 (Those seek employment overseas by themselves), the employee needs to have an employment contract legalized by the Royal Thai Embassy as part of the registration at the Department of Employment, the Ministry of Labour of Thailand at least 15 days before departure from Thailand.
Procedure to Legalize an Employment Contract
For Thai nationals going to work in Kenya, Tanzania, Ethiopia, Seychelles, Uganda, South Sudan, Rwanda, Somalia, DR Congo, Burundi, and Comoros
																		
- The Employer or the Employee sends the draft Employment Contract (example of the employment contract in the attachment) to the Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi (E-mail address:
visa.nib@mfa.go.th (mailto:visa.nib@mfa.go.th)
). The Employer is also requested to forward the business license together with the photos of the working place and the accommodation to be provided by the Employer for the Employee for the Embassy’s consideration. The Embassy shall reserve the right to visit the working place, if deemed necessary.
- In reviewing the draft employment contract, the Embassy will take into consideration the contents particularly; wage, welfare, holiday and leave, working time, overtime and holiday payment, termination of contract, air ticket fee from/to Thailand, etc. in compliance with the local Labour Law.
- In case that the Employer refuses to amend the draft employment contract as informed by the Embassy and the Employee agrees to sign that contract, the Employee is obliged to write an official Consent Letter (see the draft Consent Letter in the attachment) to the Embassy notifying that he/she will take full responsibility for his/her signing and will not claim or request for any assistance from the Embassy demanding for more benefits/interests which are not stated in the signed contract.
- Once the Employer and the Employee have both signed the employment contract, the Employer has to submit the signed original contract to the Notary Public or Chamber of Commerce or competent authority in his/her country, and then to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of his/her country for legalization. After that the signed and authorised employment contract can be submitted further to the Embassy for legalizing the signature of the officer of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Please note that original employment contract is required. Scanned file sent to the Embassy by Email cannot be accepted.
- The consular officer has the authority in rejecting legalization of employment contract if there is any indication of labour exploitation in the contents of that contract.
- Both the Employee and the Employer are advised to sign or initial their names in each page of the employment contract in order to avoid any question that may occur especially concerning the correction of the figures, such as the amount of salary or the period of employment.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
In order to protect Thai nationals who wish to work overseas and prevent the situation where Thai nationals are brought to work overseas without payment, according to the Thai Labour Act B.E. 2528 article 48 (Those seek employment overseas by themselves), the employee needs to have an employment contract legalized by the Royal Thai Embassy as part of the registration at the Department of Employment, the Ministry of Labour of Thailand at least 15 days before departure from Thailand.
Procedure to Legalize an Employment Contract
For Thai nationals going to work in Kenya, Tanzania, Ethiopia, Seychelles, Uganda, South Sudan, Rwanda, Somalia, DR Congo, Burundi, and Comoros
																		
- The Employer or the Employee sends the draft Employment Contract (example of the employment contract in the attachment) to the Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi (E-mail address:
visa.nib@mfa.go.th (mailto:visa.nib@mfa.go.th)
). The Employer is also requested to forward the business license together with the photos of the working place and the accommodation to be provided by the Employer for the Employee for the Embassy’s consideration. The Embassy shall reserve the right to visit the working place, if deemed necessary.
- In reviewing the draft employment contract, the Embassy will take into consideration the contents particularly; wage, welfare, holiday and leave, working time, overtime and holiday payment, termination of contract, air ticket fee from/to Thailand, etc. in compliance with the local Labour Law.
- In case that the Employer refuses to amend the draft employment contract as informed by the Embassy and the Employee agrees to sign that contract, the Employee is obliged to write an official Consent Letter (see the draft Consent Letter in the attachment) to the Embassy notifying that he/she will take full responsibility for his/her signing and will not claim or request for any assistance from the Embassy demanding for more benefits/interests which are not stated in the signed contract.
- Once the Employer and the Employee have both signed the employment contract, the Employer has to submit the signed original contract to the Notary Public or Chamber of Commerce or competent authority in his/her country, and then to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of his/her country for legalization. After that the signed and authorised employment contract can be submitted further to the Embassy for legalizing the signature of the officer of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Please note that original employment contract is required. Scanned file sent to the Embassy by Email cannot be accepted.
- The consular officer has the authority in rejecting legalization of employment contract if there is any indication of labour exploitation in the contents of that contract.
- Both the Employee and the Employer are advised to sign or initial their names in each page of the employment contract in order to avoid any question that may occur especially concerning the correction of the figures, such as the amount of salary or the period of employment.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Legalization of Employment Contract]
Please note that on top of the official fees mentioned above, VFS Global charges an extra service fee to paid by the applicant.

Bank: Absa Bank Kenya PLC

Account No.: 2027878655
Swift: BARCKENX

- Mpesa to Paybill 303030 (Absa Bank)– Account - 2027878655
- Mpesa to Paybill 790349 (VFS Ukraine holding) Account -Thailand
- VFS Service fee to be paid in cash in local currency at the Visa Application Centre.
Note: Paid fees are not refundable in any case.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Passport or travel document with validity not less than 6 months
- A copy of passport biodata page
- Visa application form completely filled out.
- Two photographs (passport size 3.5x4.5 cm) with a white background taken within the past six months.
- Evidence of travel from Thailand (confirmed air ticket paid in full)
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months.
- For Category TS (transit) only
- Visa of a third country in a passport or travel document  
- Letter of invitation stating the application's participation in sports activities in the Kingdom  
- Hotel booking if staying for a night
- For Category S (sport) only
- Letter of invitation stating the application's participation in sports activities in Thailand

visa_types.processing_time:
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- To travel in transit through Thailand to proceed to the country of destination or to re-enter his/her own country (category "TS")
Note: Transit less than 12 hours remaining within the restricted area in the airport does not require transit visa. Transit longer than 12 hours shall require transit visa despite the stay within restricted area.
- To participate in sports activities (sportsmen, sportswomen, etc.) within one month (category "S")
Note: Those who are scheduled to stay in the Kingdom longer than one month, please apply for Non-Immigrant Visa category “O”.
- The person in charge or crew of a conveyance coming to a port, station, or area in the Kingdom (category "C")
VALIDITY 3 months for single entry / 3 or 6 months for 2, 3, or 4 entries (not possible to issue multiple entries)
PERIOD OF STAY Not exceeding 30 days.
EXTENSION OF STAY
Those who wish to stay longer or may wish to change their type of visa must file an application for permission at the Office of Immigration Bureau located on Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210, Tel 0-2141-9889 (or at http://www.immigration.go.th (http://www.immigration.go.th)). The extension of stay as well as the change of certain type of visa is solely at the discretion of the Immigration officer.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- To travel in transit through Thailand to proceed to the country of destination or to re-enter his/her own country (category "TS")
Note: Transit less than 12 hours remaining within the restricted area in the airport does not require transit visa. Transit longer than 12 hours shall require transit visa despite the stay within restricted area.
- To participate in sports activities (sportsmen, sportswomen, etc.) within one month (category "S")
Note: Those who are scheduled to stay in the Kingdom longer than one month, please apply for Non-Immigrant Visa category “O”.
- The person in charge or crew of a conveyance coming to a port, station, or area in the Kingdom (category "C")
VALIDITY 3 months for single entry / 3 or 6 months for 2, 3, or 4 entries (not possible to issue multiple entries)
PERIOD OF STAY Not exceeding 30 days.
EXTENSION OF STAY
Those who wish to stay longer or may wish to change their type of visa must file an application for permission at the Office of Immigration Bureau located on Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210, Tel 0-2141-9889 (or at http://www.immigration.go.th (http://www.immigration.go.th)). The extension of stay as well as the change of certain type of visa is solely at the discretion of the Immigration officer.
																	

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Transit Visa]
Please note that on top of the official visa fees mentioned above, VFS Global charges an extra service fee to paid by the applicant.

[Table:  Transit Visa]
- Visa fee & Mandatory courier fee in Kenyan Shilling (KES) to be transferred to the following bank account (Once application & documents are verified at the Visa application Centre):
Name: VFS Kenya Limited
Bank: Absa Bank Kenya PLC
Branch: Absa Plaza
Account No.: 2027878655
Swift: BARCKENX
OR
- Mpesa to Paybill 303030 (Absa Bank)– Account - 2027878655
- Mpesa to Paybill 790349 (VFS Ukraine holding) Account -Thailand
- VFS Service fee to be paid in cash in local currency at the Visa Application Centre.
Note: Paid fees are not refundable in any case.

## Documents Required
- Passport or travel document with validity not less than 6 months
- A copy of passport biodata page
- Visa application form completely filled out.
- Two photographs (passport size 3.5x4.5 cm) with a white background taken within the past six months.
- Evidence of travel from Thailand (confirmed air ticket paid in full)
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months.
- For Category TS (transit) only
- Visa of a third country in a passport or travel document  
- Letter of invitation stating the application's participation in sports activities in the Kingdom  
- Hotel booking if staying for a night
- For Category S (sport) only
- Letter of invitation stating the application's participation in sports activities in Thailand

## Processing Time
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya.

Visa application form (fillable) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4oJLO9g2MEzOa11XgaW5hI/b5858bea47c5f9dca9e5dcb0a34f7efe/Visa_Form_fillable.pdf)

visa_types.name: Thai Police Clearance Certificate
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Thai Police Clearance Certificate is to be applied directly at The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya. Please click here (https://nairobi.thaiembassy.org/th/publicservice/thai-police-clearance-certificate?page=5fbf85f69d092f1e1f4fced2&menu=5d752a3215e39c4624002db4) for more information.

Thai Police Clearance Certificate is to be applied directly at The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya. Please click here (https://nairobi.thaiembassy.org/th/publicservice/thai-police-clearance-certificate?page=5fbf85f69d092f1e1f4fced2&menu=5d752a3215e39c4624002db4) for more information.

visa_types.name: Non-Immigrant Visa “O–A” (Long Stay 10 years)
visa_types.documents_required:
- Passport with validity of not less than 18 months
- 3 copies of passport biodata page
- 3 copies of completed visa application forms
- 3 passport-sized photos (4 x 6 cm) of the applicant taken within the past six months
- Accommodation/Hotel booking confirmation
- One-way or round-trip (if applicable) flight booking confirmation
- Additional personal data form (See attachment)
- Bank statement or evidence of adequate finance showing
– a deposit of the amount equal to and not less than 800,000 Baht; or
– an income certificate (an original copy) with a monthly income of not less than 65,000 Baht; or
– a deposit account plus a monthly income totalling not less than 800,000 Baht.
– In the case of submitting a bank statement, a letter of guarantee from the bank (an original copy) is required.
- Criminal record
– Letter of verification stating that the applicant has no criminal record
– It shall be valid for not more than three months and should be notarised by the applicant’s diplomatic or consular mission.
- Medical certificate
- Form is in the attachment. It must be issued from the country where the application is submitted, showing no prohibitive diseases as indicated in the Ministerial Regulation No.14 (B.E. 2535)
- It shall be valid for not more than three months and should be notarised by the applicant’s diplomatic or consular mission before submission.
- Insurance
– Applicant must have a health insurance for the duration of stay, with coverage for covid-19 disease, and with the total sum insured of THB 3,000,000 (100,000 USD) per policy year.
- In case of a foreign insurance company, the applicant must submit the original insurance policy document with 2 copies.
- In case of a Thai insurance company, the applicant must submit 2 copies of the insurance policy document or, if available, the original insurance policy document with 2 copies.
- A list of Thai insurance companies participating in the scheme can be found at http://longstay.tgia.org (http://longstay.tgia.org)
- Foreign Insurance Certificate (See attachment) must be completed, signed and stamped by the insurance company.

However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This type of visa may be issued to applicants aged 50 years and over who wish to stay in Thailand for a period of not exceeding 1 year without the intention of working. Employment of any kind is strictly prohibited.
ELIGIBILITY
- Applicant must be aged 50 years and over (on the day of submitting application).
- Applicant not prohibited from entering the Kingdom as provided by the Immigration Act B.E. 2522 (1979).
- Having no criminal record in Thailand and the country of the applicant’s nationality or residence.
- Having the nationality of or permanent residence in the country where application is submitted.
- Not having prohibitive diseases (Leprosy, Tuberculosis, drug addiction, Elephantiasis, third phase of Syphilis) as indicated in the Ministerial Regulation No. 14 B.E. 2535.
- Employment in Thailand is prohibited.
VALIDITY 1 year for multiple entries
PERIOD OF STAY 1 Not exceeding 1 year.
Recommendations for foreigners with Non-Immigrant Visa “O-A” (Long Stay/Retirement) while staying in Thailand
- Upon arrival, holder of this type of visa will be permitted to stay in Thailand for 1 year from the date of first entry.
- At the end of the 90-day stay, the foreigner must report to the immigration officer in his or her residence area and report again every 90 days during his or her stay in Thailand.  The foreigner may report to the police station if there is no immigration office in his or her residence area.
- Foreigner may report to the competent authority by post and should provide the following:
- A report form (Tor Mor 47)
- A copy of passport pages showing the foreigner’s photo, personal details, and the latest arrival visa stamp
- A copy of the previous receipt of acknowledgement
- A self-addressed envelope with postage affixed.
Such documents must be sent to the Immigration Division 1, 120 Moo 3, Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210 and must be submitted 7 days before the end of every 90-day period.  A receipt of acknowledgement will be given and should be used for future correspondence. 
- Foreigner who wishes to extend his or her stay shall submit a request for extension of stay at the Office of the Immigration Bureau with documented evidence of money transfer or a deposit account in Thailand or an income certificate showing an amount of not less than 800,000 Baht
an income certificate plus a deposit account showing a total amount of not less than 800,000 Baht.  A one-year extension of stay shall be granted at the discretion of the immigration officer to the foreigner as long as he or she meets the above requirements.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This type of visa may be issued to applicants aged 50 years and over who wish to stay in Thailand for a period of not exceeding 1 year without the intention of working. Employment of any kind is strictly prohibited.
ELIGIBILITY
- Applicant must be aged 50 years and over (on the day of submitting application).
- Applicant not prohibited from entering the Kingdom as provided by the Immigration Act B.E. 2522 (1979).
- Having no criminal record in Thailand and the country of the applicant’s nationality or residence.
- Having the nationality of or permanent residence in the country where application is submitted.
- Not having prohibitive diseases (Leprosy, Tuberculosis, drug addiction, Elephantiasis, third phase of Syphilis) as indicated in the Ministerial Regulation No. 14 B.E. 2535.
- Employment in Thailand is prohibited.
VALIDITY 1 year for multiple entries
PERIOD OF STAY 1 Not exceeding 1 year.
Recommendations for foreigners with Non-Immigrant Visa “O-A” (Long Stay/Retirement) while staying in Thailand
- Upon arrival, holder of this type of visa will be permitted to stay in Thailand for 1 year from the date of first entry.
- At the end of the 90-day stay, the foreigner must report to the immigration officer in his or her residence area and report again every 90 days during his or her stay in Thailand.  The foreigner may report to the police station if there is no immigration office in his or her residence area.
- Foreigner may report to the competent authority by post and should provide the following:
- A report form (Tor Mor 47)
- A copy of passport pages showing the foreigner’s photo, personal details, and the latest arrival visa stamp
- A copy of the previous receipt of acknowledgement
- A self-addressed envelope with postage affixed.
Such documents must be sent to the Immigration Division 1, 120 Moo 3, Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210 and must be submitted 7 days before the end of every 90-day period.  A receipt of acknowledgement will be given and should be used for future correspondence. 
- Foreigner who wishes to extend his or her stay shall submit a request for extension of stay at the Office of the Immigration Bureau with documented evidence of money transfer or a deposit account in Thailand or an income certificate showing an amount of not less than 800,000 Baht
an income certificate plus a deposit account showing a total amount of not less than 800,000 Baht.  A one-year extension of stay shall be granted at the discretion of the immigration officer to the foreigner as long as he or she meets the above requirements.

– Applicant must have a health insurance for the duration of stay, with coverage for covid-19 disease, and with the total sum insured of THB 3,000,000 (100,000 USD) per policy year.
- In case of a foreign insurance company, the applicant must submit the original insurance policy document with 2 copies.
- In case of a Thai insurance company, the applicant must submit 2 copies of the insurance policy document or, if available, the original insurance policy document with 2 copies.
- A list of Thai insurance companies participating in the scheme can be found at http://longstay.tgia.org (http://longstay.tgia.org)
- Foreign Insurance Certificate (See attachment) must be completed, signed and stamped by the insurance company.

## Processing Time
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
For All Categories:
																		
- Passport or travel document with validity not less than 6 months
- A copy of passport biodata page
- Visa application form completely filled out.
- Two photographs (passport size 3.5x4.5 cm) with a white background taken within the past six months.
- Proof of employment (the Business Registration Services, Certificate of Incorporation, Certificate of Registration, or equivalent, must be certified by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the issuing country) or student ID card (if applicable)
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months (in case of using immediate family member's bank statement, proof of kinship such as marriage certificate or birth certificate must also be attached)
- Round-trip flight booking confirmation (duly paid)
Only for Category "TR":
- Hotel booking confirmation (duly paid) or proof of accommodation in Thailand such as invitation letter from family or friend in Thailand with copies of passport or Thai national ID and work/stay permit (in case of non-Thai national)
Only for Category "MT":
ONLY for those with special cases approved by the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand 
																		
- Letter from the hospital in Thailand
- Document proving relationship between the patient and attendants as immediate family (if applicable)
- Proof of accommodation in Thailand such as hotel booking or invitation letter from family or friend in Thailand (in case of staying in the hospital, the letter from the hospital must mention that accommodation is provided in the hospital)
For Minor Child:
- A birth certificate (original and a copy /or a notarised copy)
- A letter of consent from a parent who will not be traveling or from both parents if both are not traveling (original and a copy /or a notarised copy)

visa_types.processing_time:
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- For tourism purposes (category "TR")
- To seek a medical treatment in Thailand within 60 days (category "MT")
Note: Those who are scheduled to seek medical treatment in Thailand longer than 60 days, please apply for Non-Immigrant Visa category “O” for the stay not exceeding 90 days.
- For Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions (category “MICE”) supported by Thailand Convention and Exhibition Bureau (TCEB) only upon instruction from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Thailand
VALIDITY 3 months for single entry / 6 months for multiple entries (starting from the day that visa is issued)
PERIOD OF STAY Not exceeding 60 days for single entry / Not exceeding 60 days for each stay for multiple entries (starting from the day that you arrive in Thailand)
Extension of stay: Those who wish to stay longer or may wish to change their type of visa must file an application for permission at the Office of Immigration Bureau located on Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210, Tel 0-2141-9889 (or at http://www.immigration.go.th (http://www.immigration.go.th)). The extension of stay as well as the change of certain type of visa is solely at the discretion of the Immigration officer.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- For tourism purposes (category "TR")
- To seek a medical treatment in Thailand within 60 days (category "MT")
Note: Those who are scheduled to seek medical treatment in Thailand longer than 60 days, please apply for Non-Immigrant Visa category “O” for the stay not exceeding 90 days.
- For Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions (category “MICE”) supported by Thailand Convention and Exhibition Bureau (TCEB) only upon instruction from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Thailand
VALIDITY 3 months for single entry / 6 months for multiple entries (starting from the day that visa is issued)
PERIOD OF STAY Not exceeding 60 days for single entry / Not exceeding 60 days for each stay for multiple entries (starting from the day that you arrive in Thailand)
Extension of stay: Those who wish to stay longer or may wish to change their type of visa must file an application for permission at the Office of Immigration Bureau located on Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210, Tel 0-2141-9889 (or at http://www.immigration.go.th (http://www.immigration.go.th)). The extension of stay as well as the change of certain type of visa is solely at the discretion of the Immigration officer.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Tourist Visa]
Please note that on top of the official visa fees mentioned above, VFS Global charges an extra service fee to paid by the applicant.

[Table:  Tourist Visa]
- Visa fee & Mandatory courier fee in Kenyan Shilling (KES) to be transferred to the following bank account (Once application & documents are verified at the Visa application Centre):
Name: VFS Kenya Limited
Bank: Absa Bank Kenya PLC
Branch: Absa Plaza
Account No.: 2027878655
Swift: BARCKENX
OR
- Mpesa to Paybill 303030 (Absa Bank)– Account - 2027878655
- Mpesa to Paybill 790349 (VFS Ukraine holding) Account -Thailand
- VFS Service fee to be paid in cash in local currency at the Visa Application Centre.
Note: Paid fees are not refundable in any case.

## Documents Required
For All Categories:
																		
- Passport or travel document with validity not less than 6 months
- A copy of passport biodata page
- Visa application form completely filled out.
- Two photographs (passport size 3.5x4.5 cm) with a white background taken within the past six months.
- Proof of employment (the Business Registration Services, Certificate of Incorporation, Certificate of Registration, or equivalent, must be certified by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the issuing country) or student ID card (if applicable)
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months (in case of using immediate family member's bank statement, proof of kinship such as marriage certificate or birth certificate must also be attached)
- Round-trip flight booking confirmation (duly paid)
Only for Category "TR":
- Hotel booking confirmation (duly paid) or proof of accommodation in Thailand such as invitation letter from family or friend in Thailand with copies of passport or Thai national ID and work/stay permit (in case of non-Thai national)
Only for Category "MT":
ONLY for those with special cases approved by the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand 
																		
- Letter from the hospital in Thailand
- Document proving relationship between the patient and attendants as immediate family (if applicable)
- Proof of accommodation in Thailand such as hotel booking or invitation letter from family or friend in Thailand (in case of staying in the hospital, the letter from the hospital must mention that accommodation is provided in the hospital)
For Minor Child:
- A birth certificate (original and a copy /or a notarised copy)
- A letter of consent from a parent who will not be traveling or from both parents if both are not traveling (original and a copy /or a notarised copy)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya.

## Download Forms
Visa application form (fillable) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4oJLO9g2MEzOa11XgaW5hI/b5858bea47c5f9dca9e5dcb0a34f7efe/Visa_Form_fillable.pdf)

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa / Official Visa / Courtesy Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Diplomatic Visa (D / F)
- Requirements
(1) Diplomatic passport or UN passport (UN Laissez-Passer) which is equivalent to diplomatic passport
(2) A copy of passport/UNLP biodata page
(3) Duly completed visa application form with 2 photographs
(4) Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which clearly specifies the name, position and purpose of the trip
(5) Flight booking confirmation
(6) Hotel booking confirmation
Official Visa (F)
- Requirements
(1) Official passport or UN passport (UN Laissez-Passer) which is equivalent to official passport
(2) A copy of passport/UNLP biodata page
(3) Duly completed visa application form with 2 photographs
(4) Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which clearly specifies the name, position and purpose of the trip
(5) Flight booking confirmation
(6) Hotel booking confirmation
Courtesy Visa (CY)
- Requirements
(1) Passport or UN passport (UN Laissez-Passer)
(2) A copy of passport biodata page
(3) Duly completed visa application form with 2 photographs
(4) Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which clearly specifies the name, position and purpose of the trip
(5) Flight booking confirmation
(6) Hotel booking confirmation

Please note that VFS Global charges an extra service fee to paid by the applicant.

However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Diplomatic Visa (D / F)
- Purpose

(1) assumption of duty at respective diplomatic representation in Thailand (category "D"), or
(2) a temporary entry into Thailand to perform diplomatic, consular, or official duties (category "F")
- Validity single entry or multiple entries for 3 or 6 months
- Period of Stay Not exceeding 90 days.
Official Visa (F)
- Purpose
 
A temporary entry into Thailand to perform official duties.
- Validity
 
Single entry or multiple entries for 3 or 6 months
- Period of Stay
 
Not exceeding 90 days.
Courtesy Visa (CY)
- Purpose

(1) Other purposes apart from diplomatic or official duties for the holders of diplomatic or official passport, or a UN passport equivalent to a diplomatic or official passport, or
(2) Holders of ordinary passport or travel document who enter Thailand as a royal visitor, guest of government or government agency.
- Validity 30 days with single entry or 90 days with multiple entries
- Period of Stay Not exceeding 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Diplomatic Visa (D / F)
- Purpose

(1) assumption of duty at respective diplomatic representation in Thailand (category "D"), or
(2) a temporary entry into Thailand to perform diplomatic, consular, or official duties (category "F")
- Validity single entry or multiple entries for 3 or 6 months
- Period of Stay Not exceeding 90 days.
Official Visa (F)
- Purpose
 
A temporary entry into Thailand to perform official duties.
- Validity
 
Single entry or multiple entries for 3 or 6 months
- Period of Stay
 
Not exceeding 90 days.
Courtesy Visa (CY)
- Purpose

(1) Other purposes apart from diplomatic or official duties for the holders of diplomatic or official passport, or a UN passport equivalent to a diplomatic or official passport, or
(2) Holders of ordinary passport or travel document who enter Thailand as a royal visitor, guest of government or government agency.
- Validity 30 days with single entry or 90 days with multiple entries
- Period of Stay Not exceeding 90 days.

## Visa Fees
- No Official visa fees.
Please note that VFS Global charges an extra service fee to paid by the applicant.

[Table:  Diplomatic Visa / Official Visa / Courtesy Visa]

Note: Paid fees are not refundable in any case.

## Documents Required

- Requirements

(2) A copy of passport/UNLP biodata page
(3) Duly completed visa application form with 2 photographs
(4) Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which clearly specifies the name, position and purpose of the trip
(5) Flight booking confirmation

(3) Duly completed visa application form with 2 photographs
(4) Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which clearly specifies the name, position and purpose of the trip
(5) Flight booking confirmation
(6) Hotel booking confirmation
Courtesy Visa (CY)
- Requirements
(1) Passport or UN passport (UN Laissez-Passer)
(2) A copy of passport biodata page
(3) Duly completed visa application form with 2 photographs
(4) Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs or the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which clearly specifies the name, position and purpose of the trip
(5) Flight booking confirmation
(6) Hotel booking confirmation

## Processing Time
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya.

visa_types.name: Non-Immigrant Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
For All Categories:
- Passport or travel document with validity not less than 6 months
- A copy of passport biodata page
- Visa application form completely filled out.
- Two photographs (passport size 3.5x4.5 cm) with a white background taken within the past six months.
- One-way or round-trip (if applicable) flight booking confirmation.
- Accommodation/Hotel booking confirmation.
Additional requirements for each category:
Those going to assume position in Thailand (Category "F")
- Letter from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Embassy of the applicant’s country, or the agency or organization of the UN, which must clearly specify the name, position and purpose of the trip.
Those going to attend a meeting with the Royal Thai Government, foreign government or International Organization in Thailand (Category "F")
- Letter from applicant’s company/ university/ institution indicating the applicant’s position, length of employment, purpose of visit to Thailand, length of stay in Thailand, and the financial coverage for the trip (if applicable)
- Letter of invitation from Thailand indicating length of stay (period of stay), purpose of visit to Thailand, and the financial coverage for the trip (if applicable)
Those going to work in Thailand (Category "B")
(Note: Standard Process for foreigners who wish to work in Thailand as shown below)
- Cover letter from the company in Thailand specifying employment detail including position, salary, and employment period.
- Copy of Work Permit issued by the Ministry of Labour (only in case the applicant has previously worked in Thailand) OR Letter of approval from the Ministry of Labour (Form WP.3)
Note: To obtain this letter of approval, the prospective employer in Thailand is required to submit Form WP3 at the Office of Foreign Workers Administration, Department of Employment, Ministry of Labour Tel 02-2452745, 02-2453209 or at a Provincial Employment Office in the respective province.
- Employment contract indicating rationale for employing the applicant as well as his/her salary, position, and qualifications (document must be signed by authorized managing director and affixed the seal of the company)
- Copy of corporate documents; namely (1) list of shareholders (2) business registration and business license (3) company profile (4) details of business operation (5) list of foreign workers stating names, nationalities and positions (6) map indicating the location of the company (7) balance sheet, statement of Income Tax and Business Tax (Por Ngor Dor 50 and Por Ngor Dor 30) of the latest year (8) Alien income tax return (Por Ngor Dor 91) and (9) Value-added tax registration (Por Ngor Dor 20), etc.
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months.
- Copy of educational records of the applicant and letters of recommendation from the prior employers, identifying job description and length of service time (if any)
Those going to work as teacher in Thailand (Category "B")
- Letter confirming the employment from a educational institution in Thailand
- Letter from the Office of the Private Education Commission (OPEC) or the hiring government agency

Note: Teachers at the international schools is exempted for the letter from OPEC.
- Copy of the educational institution's registration papers
- Employment contract indicating salary, position, and qualifications.
- Copy of educational records of the applicant
- Police clearance from the applicant's country of origin
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months.
Those going to conduct business in Thailand (Category "B")
- Letter from the applicant’s company indicating the applicant’s position, length of employment, salary, and purpose of visit(s) to Thailand.
- Documents showing correspondence with business partners in Thailand.
- Evidence of financial status in the case where the applicant is self-employed.
- Letter of invitation from trading or associated partners/companies in Thailand
- Corporate documents of the company in Thailand, such as business registration and business license issued by the Ministry of Commerce of Thailand, list of shareholders, company profile, details of business operation, list of foreign workers stating names, nationalities and positions, map indicating location of the company and balance sheet, statement of income tax and business (Por Ngor For 50 and Por For Ngor 30 of the latest year)
- Original bank statement of the applicant or a printout with official stamp of the bank showing a balance of at least 1,000 USD per person or 2,000 USD per family in equivalent during the past six months.
Those going to study in Thailand (Category "ED")
- Letter of acceptance from the concerned school/university or institute in Thailand
- Letter from the OPEC or local educational office where the school is located

Note: Students at the international schools or universities are exempted from this requirement.
- Copy of the educational institution's registration papers
- Evidence of financial support for the study in Thailand
Those going to attend a conference, workshop, or training course in Thailand (Category "ED")
- Letter from applicant’s company/ university/ institution indicating the applicant’s position, length of employment, purpose of visit to Thailand, length of stay in Thailand, and the financial coverage for the trip (if applicable)
- Letter of invitation from Thailand indicating length of stay (period of stay), purpose of visit to Thailand, and the financial coverage for the trip (if applicable)
- Evidence of financial support such as bank statement (if not supported by the company or institution above)
Those going to accompany family members to Thailand (Category "O")
- Document proving relationship with concerned family member, such as birth certificate or marriage certificate

Note: Documents in foreign languages must be translated into Thai or English and properly notarized by notary organs or the applicant’s diplomatic/consular mission.
- Evidence of financial support for the stay in Thailand
Those going to volunteer without paid in Thailand (Category "O")
- Cover letter from the host organization confirming the position as volunteer, including copy of the registration paper in Thailand and ID copy (if Thai) or passport copy and work permit (if non-Thai) of the signee.
- Document from the Thai government agency responsible for the host organization approving the volunteer position.
- Evidence of financial support for the stay in Thailand
Those going to conduct scientific research or training or teaching in a research institute (Category "RS")
- Letter from the National Research Council of Thailand

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- to perform official duties such as those assuming position at the international organization's office in Thailand or students under the Thailand International Cooperation Agency (TICA)'s scholarship programmes, or to attend meetings with government of Thailand, foreign government, or International Organization in Thailand (Category "F")
- to conduct business / to work (Category "B")
- to study, to have a work study or observation tour, to participate in projects or seminars, or to attend a conference, workshop, or training course (Category "ED")
- to work as a film-producer, journalist, or reporter (Category "M")
- to perform missionary work or other religious activities with the concurrence of the Thai Ministries or Government Departments concerned (Category "R")
- to conduct scientific research or training or teaching in a research institute (Category "RS")
- to undertake skilled work or to work as an expert or specialist (Category "EX")
- other activities (Category "O") as follows: to stay with the family, to perform duties for the state enterprise or social welfare organizations (volunteer without paid), to stay after retirement for the elderly (long stay), to receive medical treatment longer than 60 days, to be a sport coach as required by the Royal Thai Government, to be a contestant or witness for the judicial process.
VALIDITY 3 months for single entry / 1 year for multiple entries
PERIOD OF STAY Not exceeding 90 days for each entry
EXTENSION OF STAY

- Those qualified persons can obtain an additional one year stay permit counting from the date of entry into Thailand pertaining to the Office of the Immigration Bureau's regulations on extension of stay. The extension of stay is at the discretion of the Immigration officer.
- Applicants wishing to stay in Thailand longer than 90 days have to file their application at the Office of Immigration Bureau located on Government Center B, Chaengwattana Soi 7, Laksi, Bangkok 10210, Tel 0-2141-9889 (or at http://www.immigration.go.th (http://www.immigration.go.th)).

Note: Students at the international schools or universities are exempted from this requirement.
- Copy of the educational institution's registration papers
- Evidence of financial support for the study in Thailand
Those going to attend a conference, workshop, or training course in Thailand (Category "ED")
- Letter from applicant’s company/ university/ institution indicating the applicant’s position, length of employment, purpose of visit to Thailand, length of stay in Thailand, and the financial coverage for the trip (if applicable)
- Letter of invitation from Thailand indicating length of stay (period of stay), purpose of visit to Thailand, and the financial coverage for the trip (if applicable)
- Evidence of financial support such as bank statement (if not supported by the company or institution above)
Those going to accompany family members to Thailand (Category "O")
- Document proving relationship with concerned family member, such as birth certificate or marriage certificate

Note: Documents in foreign languages must be translated into Thai or English and properly notarized by notary organs or the applicant’s diplomatic/consular mission.
- Evidence of financial support for the stay in Thailand
Those going to volunteer without paid in Thailand (Category "O")
- Cover letter from the host organization confirming the position as volunteer, including copy of the registration paper in Thailand and ID copy (if Thai) or passport copy and work permit (if non-Thai) of the signee.
- Document from the Thai government agency responsible for the host organization approving the volunteer position.
- Evidence of financial support for the stay in Thailand
Those going to conduct scientific research or training or teaching in a research institute (Category "RS")
- Letter from the National Research Council of Thailand

## Processing Time
The timelines given by The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya for the processing of each application is minimum 1 working week (Excluding the courier transportation time), the timelines will be counted from when the Embassy has received the application.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may vary.
The normal transportation time, one way is 5 to 6 working days for Kenya, you must calculate with the transportation time when planning for your submission.
VFS Global is involved only in non-judgmental task of visa processing and does not play any role in processing the visa application as this is entirely the prerogative of The Royal Thai Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya.
